zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Harrington於大約1390年出生在英國藍卡爾法爾頓時，她的父親Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton49歲，母親Isabel English41歲。她和Sir Richard Hudleston至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於大約1450年在英國波藍德米洛姆享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Harrington于大约1390年出生在英国藍卡爾法爾頓时，她的父亲Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton49岁，母亲Isabel English41岁。她和Sir Richard Hudleston至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于大约1450年在英国波藍德米洛姆享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Harrington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Harrington (Cumberland, Lincolnshire, Northamptonshire). The Cumberland placename derives from the Old English personal name Hæfer + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Lincolnshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Hearra + Old English connective -ing- + tūn. The Northamptonshire derives from an Old English personal name Hǣthhere + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Herendeen .
Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArrachtáin ‘descendant of Arrachtán’, a personal name from a diminutive of arrachtach ‘mighty, powerful’.
Irish: in Kerry, this name was adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hIongardail, later Ó hUrdáil, ‘descendant of Iongardal’, a personal name of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Herington
meanings: English: variant of   Harrington   or   Herrington  .

variants.name: Herrington
meanings: habitational name from Heronden in Tenterden (Kent) or Herrington in Houghton (Durham). The Kent placename may derive from an Old English personal name Hȳra + Old English connective -ing- + denn ‘pasture’, while the Durham placename may come from Old English hæring ‘rocky place’ (from hær ‘rock, heap of stones’ + the placename forming suffix -ing) or hæren ‘rocky’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Arrington
meanings: variant of Harrington with loss of initial H-.
possibly a habitational name from Arrington, a place in Cambridgeshire, named from an Old English byname, Earn(a), meaning ‘eagle’ + -inga- ‘people or followers of’ + tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Harrington was born about 1390, in Farleton, Lancashire, England, her father, Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton, was 49 and her mother, Isabel English, was 41. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Sir Richard Hudleston. She died about 1450, in Millom, Cumberland, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Harrington est née environ 1390, à Farleton, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Isabel English, avait 41 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Sir Richard Hudleston. Elle est décédée environ 1450, à Millom, Cumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Harrington um 1390 in Farleton, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton 49 und ihre Mutter Isabel English 41. Sie hatte mit Sir Richard Hudleston mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb um 1450 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Millom, Cumberland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Harrington è nata il circa 1390 nel Farleton, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton, aveva 49 anni e sua madre, Isabel English, aveva 41 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Sir Richard Hudleston. Morì circa 1450, nel Millom, Cumberland, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Harringtonが約1390年にイギリスランカシュアイアファルレトンで生まれた時，父Sir Nicholas Harington of Farletonは49歳，母Isabel Englishは41歳であった。彼女はSir Richard Hudlestonとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1450年にイギリスクウンバーランドミロムで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Harrington(이)가 대략 1390년 영국 랭카실르 패를레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel English(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 Sir Richard Hudleston(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1450년 영국 쿠벌랜드 미롬에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.about 1390–about 1450: tany ho any amin'ny 1390–tany ho any amin'ny 1450

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Harrington nasceu aproximadamente 1390, em Farleton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Isabel English, tinha 41 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Sir Richard Hudleston. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1450, em Millom, Cumberland, Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Harrington родилась около 1390 г. в местности фарлетон, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel English, 41. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Sir Richard Hudleston. Она умерла около 1450 г. в местности миллом, кумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

sm.about 1390–about 1450: e tusa o le 1390–e tusa o le 1450

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Harrington nació aproximadamente 1390, en Farleton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Nicholas Harington of Farleton, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Isabel English, tenía 41 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Sir Richard Hudleston. Murió aproximadamente 1450, en Millom, Cumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
